about.title: About us
about.subtitle: Get to know us!
rina: Rina Plata is a passionate frontend developer, cat lover and proud Wayuu indigenous from San Juan del Cesar, La Guajira, Colombia, who not only shines in the technological world as a software engineering student at the Tecnológico de Antioquia and working as a developer at Enerbit, but is also the driving force behind this initiative.

In addition to her outstanding professional performance, Rina is an Ambassador of WIDS (Women in Data Science) Medellín and Women Techmakers, committed to promoting female participation in data science and technology. She is an active member of the Tech Community and PionerasDev communities, sharing valuable knowledge and experiences.

Guided by her love for her culture and roots, Rina proudly leads Guardianes Ancestrales, an event that exists thanks to her vision and desire to bring knowledge and support to her community in La Guajira. This project is dedicated to preserving ancestral knowledge and represents a significant effort to strengthen cultural and educational ties.
Her commitment extends to the project API Colombia, where she contributes to public access to valuable information about the country through a RESTful API.

Tech event organizer and speaker, Rina generously shares her experience, leaving an inspiring mark on the tech community. Her story is a testament to passion, dedication, and the ability to make a difference, not only in the tech world, but also in the positive impact she can have in her own community.
Check here Rina's talk in JSConf 2023: 'My experience as a Wayuu developer in the tech industry'

team.title: Team
team.intro: Behind Rina there is a great team that works voluntarily to make this initiative possible and bring joy to children from indigenous communities across Colombia.
team.thanks: Endless thanks to Miguel Teherán, Carolina Castañeda, Valentina Pinzón, Sandy Atencio, Sara Nieto, Valentina Ariza, Vrilli Castro, Elizabeth Rangel, Verónica Rodríguez, Kaky Rodríguez, Maris Botero, María 'Maka' Hernández, Greysy Rodríguez, Marian Villa, Rosario Casas, Manuela Castrillón, Sebastián Vargas, and many more people who support us every day with a grain of sand.

edition0.description: We raised more than 2,800,000 Colombian pesos through Vaki to bring school supplies to the children of the Wayuú Curari community in La Guajira. This was Guardianes Ancestrales' first step.

edition1.description: We held the first technology event inside an indigenous resguardo. We donated a full library and a technology kiosk, and followed up with the community for over a year.

edition2.description: We delivered books to children in a rural village in the municipality of Alejandría, bringing educational materials to those who need them most.

edition3.description: We returned to the resguardo where we held the first technology event to deliver Starlink connectivity, continuing the impact that began in 2024.

section.title: Support us
section.description: Do you want to be part of this project and help change the lives of children in indigenous communities? You can support us in many ways:

money.title: Money
money.description:
You can send financial donations from $5,000 COP through Vaki or Nequi by scanning the QR code you find here.
We have reached 16% of our $4,000,000 COP goal. All funds will be invested in educational materials donated on the day of the event.

items.title: Items
items.description:
Donate books, clothing, school supplies, or educational materials.
To coordinate delivery (in Medellín, Colombia) send us an email to guardianesancestralesdev@gmail.com or send us a message through Instagram.
Find here a list of donation ideas.

sponsorship.title: Sponsorship
sponsorship.description:
Does your company want to be part of this project?
Contact us at guardianesancestralesdev@gmail.com to learn about our available sponsorship plans.

event.title: Guardianes Ancestrales
event.intro: An event designed for the children of the Emberá community, in Medellín, Colombia.
event.description: On May 30, 2026, we will hold a day of interactive workshops, cultural activities, and donation delivery. Our goal is to bring education and technology closer to the Emberá children who will participate in this gathering.
event.donate: Your donation makes this day possible. Every contribution counts.

donations.question: Where can I send donations?
donations.answer: You can bring donations to the technology events we are present at, we will announce them on our Instagram. You can also send them to various physical addresses in Medellín-Colombia, write to us on our Instagram or email and we will coordinate the delivery.

hygiene.question: Can I donate personal hygiene products?
hygiene.answer: Yes, with the exception of menstrual cups, you can donate any personal hygiene product.

clothes1.question: Can I donate used clothes?
clothes1.answer: Yes, if the garment is in good condition.

clothes2.question: Can I donate clothes for different ages?
clothes2.answer: Yes, you can also donate adult clothing.

books1.question: Can I donate just one book?
books1.answer: Of course! All help adds up, if you have a book, a notebook that you don't use, a calculator, a shirt, each element adds up.

books2.question: What type of books can I donate?
books2.answer: All types of books, the objective is to give a library for the children since they do not have access to this type of educational material, some ideas are: mathematics books, science, technology, literature, educational material so that children learn to read and write, dictionaries.

toys.question: Can I donate toys?
toys.answer: Yes, we receive new or used toys that are in good condition.

money.question: Can I donate money?
money.answer: Yes, no matter the value, your contribution can change lives. We have several alternatives for monetary donations, you can donate through our Vaki or Nequi using our QR code.

hero.subtitle: Education, identity and technology in service of Colombia's indigenous communities.

story.title: Indigenous Colombia
story.intro: Colombia has more than 115 recognized indigenous peoples. Many of them live far from cities, with limited access to education, resources, and opportunities.
story.description: Guardianes Ancestrales works directly with these communities. We bring workshops, donations, and cultural activities to children who deserve to learn without forgetting who they are.
